name: Speedy Cash
description_short: Speedy Cash offers payday loans up to $425, lines of credit up to $4,000, and title loans up to $4,000 with same-day in-store service at Memphis locations.
description_long:
Speedy Cash operates as a short-term lending storefront with multiple locations across Memphis, Tennessee. The company has been positioned in the emergency lending market, offering rapid access to cash through physical retail stores where customers can complete applications in person. The Memphis Summer Avenue location exemplifies their standard storefront model, operating extended hours (9am-7pm most weekdays) to accommodate working customers seeking fast funding.

The company's core offerings include payday loans capped at $425 with repayment due on the customer's next payday, lines of credit extending up to $4,000 that allow flexible borrowing and repayment over time, and title loans up to $4,000 secured by vehicle equity. Beyond lending, they provide ancillary money services including check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and Green Dot prepaid card services. All loan products require in-store visits for application completion, vehicle appraisal (for title loans), and document verification including government ID, proof of income, and proof of address.

Speedy Cash distinguishes itself through transparent pricing disclosure prior to loan agreement signing, in-person customer service with trained staff to explain loan terms and answer questions during the application process, and the option to start applications online before completing them in-store. They maintain extended operating hours, including Saturday service, and operate 8 locations across the Memphis area plus Bartlett, enabling geographic accessibility. Their emphasis on the in-store experience contrasts with fully digital lenders, positioning them for customers preferring face-to-face guidance.

A factual assessment reveals Speedy Cash operates within the high-cost lending space typical of payday and title loan providers. While they provide legitimate emergency access to capital with explicit cost transparency, these products carry inherent risks: payday loans are structured for immediate repayment creating potential debt cycles, title loans risk vehicle repossession, and all products likely carry APRs substantially exceeding 36%, disqualifying them as payday alternatives. The business model depends on repeat borrowing rather than one-time emergency use, a structural characteristic of the sector.
pros: Payday loans capped at $425 with guaranteed next-payday repayment timeline and disclosed costs before signing
Lines of credit up to $4,000 with flexible draw-as-needed structure and credit limit restoration as you pay down balance
Title loans up to $4,000 for customers with vehicle equity, enabling larger emergency loans than payday products
Extended weekday hours (9am-7pm) and Saturday service (9am-4pm) accommodating working customer schedules
In-person application support with staff trained to explain loan terms, eligibility requirements, and repayment obligations
Multiple money services (check cashing, wire transfers, money orders, Green Dot cards) creating one-stop access for unbanked customers
8 Memphis-area locations plus Bartlett reducing travel distance for local customers seeking in-store service
cons: Payday loans require full repayment in 2-4 weeks, creating high default risk and potential debt cycling for customers with inconsistent income
Title loans risk vehicle repossession if borrower cannot repay, jeopardizing transportation and employment for financially vulnerable customers
No mention of APR disclosure on website; implied triple-digit rates typical of payday/title lending, far exceeding 36% CFPB guidance
In-person application requirement creates barriers for customers with transportation, mobility, or scheduling constraints versus fully online competitors
All loan products require employment/income proof, excluding unemployed, disabled, or self-employed individuals with irregular documentation
best_for: Salaried employees with stable paychecks facing small emergency expenses ($425 or less) who prefer in-person guidance and immediate funding
Vehicle owners needing emergency loans larger than payday amounts with liquid vehicle equity and ability to repay within lender's timeline
Unbanked customers seeking bundled money services (check cashing, wire transfers) alongside emergency lending at a single location
Customers uncomfortable with online applications who benefit from real-time staff answers to questions about loan terms and repayment mechanics
diagnosis: Speedy Cash is best for salaried employees or vehicle owners in the Memphis area needing emergency cash within days and preferring in-person service with staff guidance. The critical caveat is that all products carry implied APRs far exceeding 36%, creating high-cost debt risk—payday loans particularly risk debt cycles due to two-week repayment windows, and title loans risk vehicle loss for low-income borrowers unable to refinance.
services: Payday loans up to $425 with next-payday repayment
Lines of credit up to $4,000 with flexible borrowing and repayment over time
Title loans up to $4,000 secured by vehicle equity
Check cashing for payroll and personal checks
Green Dot Visa prepaid debit card services
In-person loan counseling and document review
